title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Margaret Ernst meets with Mr. Kachpur (referred to as Mr. Cattermole) at her cottage, which is filled with books and portraits of her late husband, Charles. Margaret explains that she and Charles took over the parish following the deaths of Patrick and Frances Ives. She notes that the couple was kind but lacked the funds for elaborate headstones, so she helped finance the epitaphs. Margaret mentions that she obtained information regarding the parish from Dr. Flavoward, whom she considers the only trustworthy person in Gleghowling.

During the conversation, Margaret displays a defiant nature, asserting that she will speak out even more if people attempt to silence her. While she acknowledges the danger posed by the murderer of Harriet Sipper, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nigges, she insists she is willing to talk to Kachpur regardless. When Kachpur attempts to build rapport by mentioning Victor Mink, Margaret responds that she enjoys making Mink uncomfortable by rebelling against his wishes.

Kachpur asks about the history of Patrick and Frances Ives, but Margaret refuses to discuss them immediately, stating she needs time to think. Kachpur then reveals a crucial piece of evidence: gold cufflinks engraved with the initials "Patrick J. I." were found in the mouths of the three murder victims. Margaret does not seem surprised by this discovery. When Kachpur shifts the topic to the victims Harriet, Ada, and Richard, Margaret provides extensive, detailed information about two of them without hesitation.
